---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "fastqc"
namespace: "Qc"
description: "Fastqc component, please see https://www.bioinformatics.babraham.ac.uk/projects/fastqc/."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `fastqc`  
Namespace: `qc`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/qc/fastqc)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/qc/fastqc)

:::

This component can take one or more files (by means of shell globbing) or a complete directory


## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name        |Description                                                                                                         |Attributes                               |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--mode`    |The mode in which the component works. Can be either files or dir.                                                  |`string`, default: `"files"`             |
|`--input`   |Directory containing input fastq files.                                                                             |`file`, required, example: `"fastq_dir"` |
|`--output`  |Output directory to write reports to.                                                                               |`file`, required, example: `"qc"`        |
|`--threads` |Specifies the number of files which can be processed simultaneously. Each thread will be allocated 250MB of memory. |`integer`                                |

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/qc/fastqc/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/qc/fastqc).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/qc/fastqc/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
